solute.description: This molecule consists of a six-membered aromatic benzene ring directly substituted with a single carboxylic acid functional group. The structure is characterized by a planar geometry where the carboxyl group, containing both a carbonyl oxygen and a hydroxyl group, is conjugated with the pi-system of the phenyl ring. It is a small organic molecule that exhibits significant polarity and strong hydrogen bonding capacity, acting as both a hydrogen bond donor and acceptor through its acidic moiety. The molecule’s distinctive character arises from the balance between its hydrophobic aromatic core and its highly polar, ionizable carboxylic acid substituent.

solvent.description: This molecule is a linear, eight-carbon aliphatic chain known as n-octane, characterized by a completely unbranched and acyclic backbone. It is a saturated hydrocarbon consisting entirely of $sp^3$ hybridized carbon atoms and hydrogen atoms, containing no functional groups, double bonds, or heteroatoms. Due to the lack of electronegative elements, the structure is entirely non-polar and possesses no capacity for hydrogen bonding as either a donor or an acceptor. Its distinctive character lies in its uniform hydrophobic profile and its role as a medium-length straight-chain alkane.
